exercises.title: Comparison Operators
exercises.description: Comparison operators return True or False. Print the result of six comparisons between x and y.
exercises.starterCode:
x = 15
y = 25

# TODO: Print result of each comparison
print(x == y)   # equal to
print(x != y)   # not equal to
print(x < y)    # less than
print(x > y)    # greater than
print(x <= y)   # less than or equal to
print(x >= y)   # greater than or equal to
exercises.expectedOutput:
False
True
True
False
True
False
hints: == checks equality (two equal signs); = is assignment (one equal sign).
Each comparison produces exactly True or False.

exercises.title: Truthy and Falsy Values
exercises.description: Every Python value is either truthy or falsy. Use bool() to check. Print the bool() of several values to see which are truthy.
exercises.starterCode:
# TODO: Print bool() of each value to see truthy/falsy
print(bool(0))       # zero
print(bool(42))      # non-zero int
print(bool(""))      # empty string
print(bool("hi"))    # non-empty string
print(bool(None))    # None
print(bool(0.0))     # zero float
print(bool(0.1))     # non-zero float
exercises.expectedOutput:
False
True
False
True
False
False
True
hints: Falsy values in Python: 0, 0.0, '', None, [], {}, False.

exercises.title: Comparison Chains
exercises.description: Python allows chaining comparisons like math: 0 < x < 10. This is unique to Python. Print the result of chained comparisons.
exercises.starterCode:
score = 75

# TODO: Print whether score is between 60 and 100 (inclusive) using chaining
print(60 <= score <= 100)

# TODO: Print whether score is between 0 and 50
print(0 <= score <= 50)

age = 25
# TODO: Print whether age is a valid working age (18 to 65 inclusive)
print(18 <= age <= 65)

# TODO: Print: True (Booleans are subclass of int; True == 1)
print(True == 1)
print(False == 0)
hints: 60 <= score <= 100 checks that score is at least 60 AND at most 100.
True equals 1 and False equals 0 in Python — booleans are integers.

warmup.title: Quick Recall: = vs ==
warmup.description: = assigns a value; == compares two values. What does this print?
warmup.starterCode:
x = 10       # assignment
print(x == 10)  # comparison: True
print(x == 20)  # comparison: False
print(x != 5)   # not equal: True
